text: {hub_gossip}
Ran a gear check. Everything is technically present. Confidence: borderline.
Ate an expired MRE before deploying. Flavor: existential. Stomach: coping.
Gave themselves a pep talk in the locker-room mirror. Rat Rating momentarily irrelevant.
Checked the stash. Counted the water bottles. There are more water bottles than expected.
Took a recovery nap on a cot in Desperanza. Dreamed about crates. Woke up reaching for one.
Stood in the pod queue to size up the competition. Everyone avoided eye contact. Perfect.
Visited the medbay. The medic sighed before even looking up. Patched and cleared for deployment.
Spilled synth-coffee on my boots. The boots took it personally.
Mission terminal displayed an error message: 'No.'
Sat on a cot. The springs made a noise that sounded like judgment.
Waited in line at Desperanza Café. The line did not move. Time lost meaning.
Closed my locker too hard. Someone three lockers down flinched.
Found a water bottle in my stash that I definitely did not put there.
Overheard someone bragging about a raid they clearly did not survive.
Tried to use the terminal. It asked for a fingerprint. I pretended not to hear it.
Opened a sugar packet. It exploded like a tiny grenade.
Counted my ammo twice. Got two different numbers. Accepted my fate.
Sat at a café table. It wobbled violently. I wobbled with it.
Jukebox switched to static mid-song. No one reacted. This is normal.
Locked eyes with another raider across the café. Neither of us blinked.
Scrolled through mission listings for 10 minutes. Picked the first one anyway.
My locker jammed. I pretended I meant to leave it closed.
Tried to stir my drink. The spoon bent. I took the hint.
Mission terminal displayed a Before-ish fact sourced from snack packaging. Scholarship has declined, but confidence has not.
Sat in a chair backwards to look cool. Immediately regretted it.
Bought a sandwich. It tasted like disappointment and cardboard.
Waited behind someone using the terminal. They were reading every word.
Someone laughed loudly in the café. Everyone stared like it was illegal.
Closed my locker and it echoed dramatically. Felt unintentionally cool.
Terminal asked for a password. Permit Choir hummed the hint in four-part harmony. It did not help.
Sneezed in the café. Three raiders ducked for cover.
Tried to repair my gear. Made it worse. Much worse.
Pulled out a chair. It made the loudest noise imaginable.
Terminal began updating. Estimated time: 'Unknown'.
Waved at someone who wasn’t waving at me. Continued waving anyway.
Reorganized my locker. It is now worse than before.
Terminal beeped loudly. I pretended it wasn’t my fault.
Ordered hot food at Desperanza Cafe. Nearly Fresh policy classified it as temperature-adjacent.
Tried to scroll the terminal with two fingers. It zoomed instead.
Dropped my spoon. It rolled under someone else’s table. I left it there.
Slammed my locker shut. It echoed like a dramatic movie moment.
Accidentally opened the Hatch Authority appeals menu. It asked me to define leaving. Immediately panicked.
Stared into my drink for too long. It stared back.
Clicked the terminal too many times. It froze out of spite.
Sat in the café long enough to forget why I came here.
Checked my locker for loot I knew wasn’t there. It still wasn’t there.
Terminal played a loud notification. Everyone glared at me.
Ate something from the cafe menu labeled Nearly Fresh Field Trial. Regretted participating in science.
Stood at the terminal pretending to read while actually zoning out.
Smiled at someone near the pod queue. They did not smile back. Social HP took imaginary damage.
Imagined my locker full of legendary loot. Opened it. Reality disagreed.
Tapped the terminal screen harder. It did not help.
Desperanza civic desk stamped my deployment form with the force of a tiny verdict.
Passed the hydroponic greens. They looked healthier than me. I respected them and resented them.
Water dispenser produced one heroic drop. The line applauded quietly. Morale became complicated.
Security scanner asked if I had dangerous items. I held up the backpack. It waved me through.
Pawn clerk appraised a cracked mug from across the room and whispered, 'potential.' I felt seen.
Lost-and-found clerk inventoried six left gloves and one heroic spoon. None were mine. Wanted them anyway.
Mission board had a sticky note reading DO NOT CHASE HUMMING CRATES. Took a picture for ignoring later.
Cafe receipt printed a fortune: 'Today you will carry too much.' Finally, accurate service.
Gave the shield a tiny pep talk at the maintenance bench. It did not answer, which felt professional.
Coupon Barons argued over a 3% discount like it was a peace treaty. I learned nothing and respected everyone less.
Gear weirdo tried to sell me a Bravado Battery. It hummed before the pitch did.
Permit Choir recited the Ready Up liability waiver in harmony. Several clauses had choreography.
Hatch Authority stamped my deployment form APPROVED, THEN QUESTIONED. Very Desperanza. Very official.
Water Ration Desk measured my cup, my thirst, and my attitude. Only the cup passed inspection.
Security Stamp Maze inspected the backpack. It beeped at three spoons and waved through everything dangerous.
Pawn Desk updated the cracked mug index. Market confidence is irrationally high.
A Desperanza historian proved The Before-ish was peaceful using a snack wrapper and unreasonable confidence.
Two raiders argued about The Bad Years over a binder labeled DEFINITIVE, VERSION 6. Nobody won. History survived.
Civic plaque called The Great Downstairs humanity's finest tunnel-based decision. The ceiling dripped in agreement.
Departure Claims window displayed NEXT LAUNCH: YESTERDAY. The clerk said this was technically encouraging.
Snack Shrine Custodian warned me not to take the triangle crackers. Would not explain which crackers were triangles.
Desperanza Cafe offered a Nearly Fresh sample. The wrapper said BEST BY: DISCUSSION. I ate around the question mark.
Rested on a ComfortCo cot. The warranty promised emotional support and delivered a spring directly into my spine.
Pawn Desk clerk predicted my stash overflow by weighing one cracked mug and sighing professionally.
Gear weirdo tried to sell me a shield upgrade described as 'mostly theoretical, emotionally proven.' I listened too long.
Handler Certification Office handed me a Signal Conduct form. Question one asked if yelling counts as strategy.
Coupon Barons challenged my receipt to single combat. The receipt lost on a technicality and I somehow wanted more loot.
Bravado Battery Kiosk demonstrated a cell labeled COURAGEOUS. It produced one spark and a legally binding thumbs-up.
Nearly Fresh Counter made me swear the sample was food before they handed it over. Morale respected the paperwork.
OopsAll Claims Window apologized for my missing package by offering a different missing package. Progress, allegedly.
Exit Strategy Booth sold me a map with three exits and four apologies. I circled the apology nearest the LZ.
